page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server instance designed for memory-intensive and high-throughput storage workloads. It features 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors with an x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 144 physical cores supporting hyper-threading, a 504 MB L3 cache, and 2232 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz.

For storage-heavy operations, the server includes 16242 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity distributed across six local SSDs. This storage configuration, combined with the high-density DDR5 memory, delivers top-tier performance in data-intensive benchmarks. Specifically, the server achieves top-tier results in memory bandwidth tests, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 23041.86 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 412.25 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput.

While the server exhibits strong single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Passmark benchmarks, certain workloads show performance tradeoffs. For instance, Geekbench multi-core scores and LLM inference text generation speeds for a 135M model fall into the average and poor categories respectively, indicating that sequential text generation without GPU acceleration is a bottleneck. However, prompt processing for medium-sized 7B LLM models remains highly efficient, placing in the top tier.

Given its massive memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and dedicated compute resources, this bare-metal instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for enterprise-grade deployments that require massive resource density without virtualization overhead. It is highly suited for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching platforms, high-throughput web serving, and complex data compilation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a high-density bare-metal server configured with 288 dedicated vCPUs and 2232 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory. Powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors running at 4.2 GHz, this instance features 16242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six drives. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong single-core processing. However, it exhibits tradeoffs in multi-core Geekbench scaling and LLM text generation due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring massive resource density and direct hardware access, making it highly suitable for large-memory databases, high-throughput web serving, and intensive data compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 288 vCPUs, 2232 GB DDR5 RAM, and 16242 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.

tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory and local NVMe storage for demanding database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6985P-C processors at 4.2 GHz
2232 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory for memory-intensive applications
16242 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across six bundled drives
Top-tier memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis performance
Best suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and data compilation
